rationale: EE has a stable IEEE-style subdiscipline structure. Fourteen subspecialties split by core function (circuits, signals, power, control, communications) plus the modern additions (photonics, robotics-EE, wireless). I made AI Hardware (accelerators, neuromorphic) its own card because the IC community has a distinct hardware-for-AI identity now. I kept Robotics here as 'Robotics & Mechatronics' even though Robotics also sits under CS — the hardware/mechatronics flavor lives here, the perception/planning flavor lives in CS.

subspecialties.name: Circuits & Integrated Systems
covers: analog and mixed-signal IC design, RFIC, VLSI, digital design, low-power circuits, power management ICs
JSSC, TCAS, ISSCC
explicitly_excludes: computer architecture for general computing → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Semiconductor Devices & Electronics
subspecialties.tagline: Transistors, photodetectors, power devices, wide bandgap.
covers: transistor device physics, photodetectors, LED/laser devices, power semiconductor devices, wide-bandgap (GaN, SiC), 2D-material devices
T-ED, EDL

subspecialties.name: Photonics & Optoelectronics
subspecialties.tagline: Lasers, fibers, integrated photonics, displays, sensors.
covers: semiconductor lasers, fiber optics, integrated photonics, displays, optical sensors, photonic ICs, silicon photonics
JLT, Opt Express, IEEE Photon J

subspecialties.name: Signal Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Adaptive, statistical, DSP for comms, audio, sensor arrays.
covers: adaptive signal processing, statistical signal processing, array processing, speech and audio processing, sparse signal recovery, time-frequency methods
TSP, JSTSP, ICASSP

subspecialties.name: Image & Video Processing
covers: image enhancement and restoration, compression (HEVC, AV1, neural codecs), computational imaging, medical image processing methods, hyperspectral image processing
TIP, IEEE TPAMI signal-flavored

subspecialties.name: Control Systems & Robotics-EE
subspecialties.tagline: Robust, optimal, adaptive control; mechatronics; robot hardware.
covers: robust and optimal control, adaptive control, networked control systems, mechatronic systems, robot hardware design, robot actuator and sensor design
Automatica, T-AC, T-CST, T-RO hardware
explicitly_excludes: robot learning and planning → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Power & Energy Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Grid, power electronics, renewables integration, storage.
covers: power systems engineering, smart grid, power electronics, renewable energy integration, energy storage systems, electric machines, microgrids
T-PS, T-PE, T-IE
explicitly_excludes: energy policy → Public Health / Social Sciences

subspecialties.name: Wireless Communications
covers: wireless physical layer, MIMO, mmWave, massive MIMO, beamforming, 5G/6G, satellite communications, integrated sensing and communications
TWC, T-COM, JSAC
explicitly_excludes: network protocols at higher layer → Computer Science & AI (systems)

subspecialties.name: Networks, IoT & Edge Computing
subspecialties.tagline: Network protocols, IoT systems, edge compute, vehicular nets.
covers: network protocols (low-layer), IoT systems, edge computing networks, vehicular networks, optical networks, network resource allocation
explicitly_excludes: distributed systems for general compute → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Biomedical Electronics & Bioelectronics
covers: implantable devices, wearable health electronics, BCI hardware, biopotential amplifiers, neurostimulation devices, biosensor electronics
T-BME, TBioCAS

subspecialties.name: AI Hardware & Neuromorphic
subspecialties.tagline: ML accelerators, in-memory compute, neuromorphic, edge AI chips.
covers: ML accelerator design, in-memory computing, neuromorphic chips, edge AI silicon, ASIC/FPGA for ML, photonic computing
explicitly_excludes: computer architecture for general purpose → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Sensors & Instrumentation
subspecialties.tagline: MEMS, gas sensors, sensor arrays, calibration, sensor networks.
covers: MEMS sensors, gas and chemical sensors, sensor arrays, sensor calibration, sensor networks, IMU and inertial sensors

subspecialties.name: RF, Microwave & Antennas
subspecialties.tagline: Antennas, RF circuits, EM theory, propagation.
covers: antenna design, microwave circuits, RF passive components, EM theory and computation, propagation modeling, reconfigurable antennas, metasurfaces
T-AP, T-MTT

subspecialties.name: Embedded & Cyber-Physical Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Embedded SW, real-time, automotive ECU, hardware security.
covers: embedded systems, real-time systems, automotive ECU, hardware security, side-channel hardware, FPGA design from systems angle

uncovered_concerns: Considered Reconfigurable Computing / FPGA as its own card — folded under Embedded & Cyber-Physical Systems and AI Hardware.
Considered Smart City Engineering — folds under Networks, IoT & Edge Computing or under Mechanical/Civil at level 1.
Considered Quantum Hardware — most physically belongs in Physics & Astronomy (Quantum Information); when it's classical control hardware FOR quantum systems, it lives here under Control Systems.
